warnings: Several high-severity items are uncontrolled: gauge dependence of the effective potential (order-of-magnitude on Omega_GW), the xi=1 vs epsilon~0 internal tension, and four missing constraint calculations gating most cosmological/laboratory bounds.

The PTArcade campaign fits a model-only (no SMBHB foreground) signal; NANOGrav new-physics Bayes factors and preferred regions depend strongly on astrophysical-foreground modeling, which differs from the SMBHB-augmented literature best-fits.
No constraint currently excludes any part of the 1-sigma region, so the reported preferred region is provisional pending the missing calculations.
approximations: High-T (small field/T) polynomial expansion of the effective potential (FOPT backend).
No RG running and no daisy resummation; renormalization scale fixed at mu=vev.
Scalar and Goldstone contributions neglected in backend input (CW quartic fixed internally).
ksw (kinetic efficiency) = 1, justified only in the vacuum-dominated runaway limit.
dbf amplitude ~1/beta_H^2 with fixed spectral-shape exponents (backend/spectrum.py, 2511.15687).
Constraint epsilon-vs-mass bounds read as order-of-magnitude bands from a 2026 review summary, not recast.

upstream_statuses.note: Preliminary scan; model well-covered in literature (2501.11619, 2502.19478); theory uncertainties flagged.
Validated, no red flags, but 5 warnings and 3 unresolved clarification requests (lambda_Phi meaning, thermal masses/daisy/RG, xi=1 thermalization).
Backend executed; 148/157 viable, 8 failed (incl. eternal inflation), 1 not_fopt; 5 warnings, 3 approximations.
Benchmark scan + PTArcade NG15 campaign (1e5 samples) completed; dbf template; 3 warnings, 2 approximations.
Most constraints not yet applicable; 4 high-priority missing calculations; no exclusions of the 1-sigma region.

assumptions.description: Classical scale invariance: no tree-level mass term, symmetry breaking is purely radiative (Coleman-Weinberg dimensional transmutation). Required by the single-field semianalytic backend selection rule.
assumptions.required_solution: None; structural model choice validated by critic. Breaks if any tree mass is induced (see A02).

source.note: lambda_Phi is the CW-renormalized quartic at mu=v; no implicit tree mass.

assumptions.description: Higgs portal lambda_HPhi assumed negligible (<<1e-10) for PT/GW so as not to induce a tree mass and spoil conformality.
assumptions.required_solution: Enforce lambda_HPhi<<1e-10; trivially evades collider Higgs-invisible bounds (C10). But conflicts with thermalization need (see A05).

source.note: Flagged warning; propagated to constraints C10.

assumptions.description: Kinetic mixing epsilon assumed ~0 for PT/GW dynamics.
assumptions.required_solution: Compute minimum epsilon for xi=1 thermalization and intersect with collider/beam-dump/Neff bounds (missing calc M1).

source.note: epsilon cannot be exactly 0 on the cosmology side; central internal tension.

assumptions.description: Single-field, minimal (DM-free) model: only the scalon rho and dark photon A'. Literature benchmarks (2502.19478) include a dark fermion DM Yukawa absent here, affecting beta_lambda/m_rho mapping.
assumptions.required_solution: None; intended minimal model. Affects only quantitative transfer of literature benchmarks.

source.note: Drop DM Yukawa for user's model.

assumptions.description: Central internal tension: xi = T_dark/T_visible = 1 (common temperature, feeds g_* and GW redshift) requires a nonzero portal, but both lambda_HPhi and epsilon are set ~0. Sectors cannot thermalize with exactly zero portals.
assumptions.required_solution: Compute epsilon_min (or alternative thermalization mechanism) reproducing xi=1; verify against Neff (M1/M3).

source.note: Flagged by critic and constraint agents; gates C02/C03.

assumptions.description: Reheating after the PT sets a common temperature and Treh is the relevant temperature key for redshifting the GW spectrum.
assumptions.required_solution: Consistent with literature; relies on efficient reheating in vacuum-dominated transitions.

source.note: temperature_key='Treh'.

assumptions.description: Gauge dependence of the CW+thermal effective potential gives an order-of-magnitude uncertainty on Omega_GW; the backend uses a single fixed gauge (1707.06765).
assumptions.required_solution: Use a gauge-invariant method (e.g. 2602.14866) or quote the gauge-variation band; document the gauge choice.

source.note: Propagated into pta and constraints; widens preferred region.

assumptions.description: No RG running and no daisy resummation; renormalization scale fixed at mu=vev.
assumptions.required_solution: Add RG-improvement and daisy/3D-EFT resummation (literature uses RG-improved CW + daisy).

source.note: Critic clarification request #2 unresolved.

assumptions.description: High-temperature (small field/T) polynomial expansion of the effective potential.
assumptions.required_solution: Valid because the thermal barrier sits at small chi/T in strong supercooling (per backend docs); cross-check with full thermal integrals.

source.note: Critic judged valid in the intended regime.

assumptions.description: Gaussian approximation for the false-vacuum fraction / percolation.
assumptions.required_solution: Stated valid in all regimes per backend docs; verify for extreme alpha.

source.note: Percolation checked in validation block.

assumptions.description: Strong supercooling with very large alpha (up to ~1e16) and eternal-inflation/percolation failures (8 failed points). Vacuum domination and possible eternal inflation at the lowest g_D.
assumptions.required_solution: Failed points recorded and excluded; verify percolation completion and that vacuum energy is efficiently converted.

source.note: Eternal inflation flagged among failures.

assumptions.description: Spectrum template = dissipative bulk flow (dbf, 2511.15687); higgsless sound-wave template deemed outside validity domain and not used.
assumptions.required_solution: dbf is the physically motivated strong-supercooling choice; remaining uncertainty in shape/normalization between bf and dbf retained as comparison.

source.note: Template choice gates the entire posterior.

assumptions.description: Kinetic efficiency ksw=1, justified only in the vacuum-dominated runaway limit K=alpha/(1+alpha)->1.
assumptions.required_solution: Valid for alpha>>1; revisit for lower-alpha (high-g_D) edge of the scan.

source.note: Tied to supercooling assumption.

assumptions.description: dbf amplitude scaling ~1/beta_H^2 with fixed spectral-shape exponents as implemented in backend/spectrum.py.
assumptions.required_solution: Cross-check exponents/normalization against the 2511.15687 fits and simulation systematics.

source.note: Template internals.

assumptions.description: PTArcade priors: log10_v ~ Uniform(-3, 1.70) [GeV], g_D ~ Uniform(0.45, 0.90). Log prior on v, linear on g_D.
assumptions.required_solution: Posterior falls off before prior edges (no boundary contact reported); choice of log vs linear still shapes mass posteriors.

source.note: Wider than 14/14 benchmark window.

assumptions.description: Sampler/campaign settings: mode=ceffyl, N_samples=1e5, acceptance 0.385, single chain. Reduced/factorized likelihood (ceffyl) vs full PTA likelihood.
assumptions.required_solution: Validate convergence (multiple chains/Gelman-Rubin); ceffyl is an approximation to the full enterprise likelihood.

source.note: Smoke test at N=100 only for pipeline validation.

assumptions.description: Model-only signal fit (no SMBHB astrophysical-foreground component) on the NANOGrav 15yr data; HD-correlated common-process assumed to be the new-physics GWB.
assumptions.required_solution: Refit including an SMBHB foreground component (as in 2501.11619) and report Bayes factor; NANOGrav results depend strongly on foreground modeling.

source.note: Web check: NG15 new-physics conclusions depend on astrophysical-foreground assumptions; literature best-fits add SMBHB.

assumptions.description: NANOGrav 15yr dataset assumptions inherited from PTArcade/ceffyl: pulsar noise model, HD spatial correlations, frequency-bin (violin) representation, achromatic GWB.
assumptions.required_solution: Inherent to the dataset/tool; document as inherited modeling assumptions.

source.note: ceffyl uses NG15 free-spectrum representation.

assumptions.description: Four high-priority missing calculations: (1) epsilon_min for thermalization, (2) dark-photon widths/lifetime/branchings (A'->rho rho vs visible), (3) Delta Neff, (4) scalon relic + decay lifetime. They gate C02-C09.
assumptions.required_solution: Run dark_portal_thermalization, dark_photon_decay, dark_Neff, scalon_relic_decay modules.

source.note: No exclusion possible until computed; could exclude or confirm region.

assumptions.description: Scalon (lightest dark state, 11-24 MeV): with epsilon=0 it is stable and may overclose the Universe; with epsilon!=0 it decays via epsilon^4-suppressed loop with potentially long lifetime (~1e3 s), risking BBN/CMB energy injection.
assumptions.required_solution: Compute Y_rho, <sigma v>, Gamma_rho, tau_rho and injection epoch (C08/C09).

source.note: Depends on epsilon (A03).

assumptions.description: Delta Neff from a thermalized MeV-scale dark sector (A':3 + scalon:1 dof) vs Planck Neff=2.99+/-0.17; likely the strongest test of xi=1.
assumptions.required_solution: Compute dark decoupling temperature, entropy ratio and Delta Neff (M3/C02).

source.note: Gates the common-temperature assumption.

assumptions.description: Collider/beam-dump epsilon bounds at 0.3-0.7 GeV and scalon decay lifetime taken as order-of-magnitude bands from review/literature (2603.08430, 2502.19478), not recast onto this exact model; numbers read from a PDF model-summary not a numerical table.
assumptions.required_solution: Recast with darkcast / published exclusion plots once epsilon and branchings are computed.

source.note: Order-of-magnitude only.

required_followups.description: Compute minimum kinetic mixing epsilon required for xi=1 thermalization and the associated dark-photon decay widths/lifetime and A'->rho rho vs visible branchings.
required_followups.suggested_action: Run dark_portal_thermalization.py and dark_photon_decay.py; intersect epsilon with C04-C06 and the Neff window.

required_followups.description: Compute Delta Neff for the thermalized MeV-scale dark sector and confront Planck Neff=2.99+/-0.17 to test the xi=1 common-temperature assumption.
required_followups.suggested_action: Run dark_Neff.py (dark decoupling T, entropy ratio).

required_followups.description: Compute scalon relic abundance and epsilon-loop lifetime to test overclosure (C09) and BBN/CMB late-decay energy injection (C08).
required_followups.suggested_action: Run scalon_relic_decay.py for the exact 1-sigma point.

required_followups.description: Quantify and propagate the gauge-dependence theory uncertainty on Omega_GW (order of magnitude) rather than relying on a single fixed-gauge backend run.
required_followups.suggested_action: Re-evaluate alpha/beta_H/Treh with a gauge-invariant scheme (2602.14866) or report a gauge-variation band.

required_followups.description: Refit the PTArcade campaign including an SMBHB astrophysical-foreground component and report the model-vs-SMBHB Bayes factor.
required_followups.suggested_action: Add SMBHB to the PTArcade signal model (as in 2501.11619) and recompute posteriors and evidence.

required_followups.description: Add RG running and daisy/3D-EFT resummation to the effective potential; resolve critic clarification on thermal masses.
required_followups.suggested_action: Upgrade backend treatment beyond mu=vev fixed-scale, no-daisy approximation.

required_followups.description: Validate PTArcade posterior convergence with multiple chains and cross-check ceffyl against the full enterprise likelihood.
required_followups.suggested_action: Run additional chains; compute Gelman-Rubin; spot-check with full likelihood.

required_followups.description: Recast laboratory dark-photon bounds with darkcast once epsilon and branchings are known.
required_followups.suggested_action: Replace order-of-magnitude bands with model-specific exclusion curves.
